finding: Homepage stat counters render as '0+', '$0M+', '0+' — to a cold visitor with no context this reads as a fake, brand-new, or broken site, destroying trust before they read the testimonials below.

evidence.quote: stat row shows 0+ / $0M+ / 0+

0+
ENTREPRENEURS LAUNCHED
$0M+
SNACK/DRINK SALES IN VENDINGPRENEUR MACHINES
0+
VENDING LOCATIONS W/OUR GUIDANCE

persona_rationale: I arrived cold from social media and judge legitimacy in seconds. '0 entrepreneurs launched' and '$0M in sales' tells me either nobody has used this or the page is broken — either way I leave before scrolling to the proof.

suggested_fix.current: Counters animating from/stuck at 0 with no real values (0+, $0M+, 0+)
suggested_fix.proposed: Render real seeded values (e.g. '500+', '$10M+', '1,200+') server-side so they never display as zero, even before any count-up animation runs

finding: The primary nav CTA 'STEP INSIDE' does not communicate what it does (it links to /apply).

evidence.quote: STEP INSIDE

link "Step inside":
    - /url: /apply

persona_rationale: As a first-timer I scan the nav for the obvious next step. 'STEP INSIDE' is a vibe, not an action — I don't know if it logs me in, shows a tour, or asks for money, so I hesitate instead of clicking.

suggested_fix.proposed: 'APPLY NOW' (match the hero CTA so the primary action is named consistently)

finding: Two differently-labelled CTAs ('STEP INSIDE' in the nav and 'APPLY NOW' in the hero) both link to /apply, which is ambiguous about whether they lead to the same place.

evidence.quote: STEP INSIDE
APPLY NOW

persona_rationale: Different labels imply different destinations. I'd waste a moment wondering if 'STEP INSIDE' is something other than applying, which is friction I won't tolerate.

suggested_fix.current: 'STEP INSIDE' (nav) and 'APPLY NOW' (hero) both go to /apply
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one consistent label 'APPLY NOW' for both, or differentiate destinations if they are meant to differ

finding: The homepage has horizontal scroll at mobile-375, making the landing page feel broken on the phone a social-media visitor arrives with.

evidence.quote: mobile-375 | home-003-mobile.png | horizontal scroll present

persona_rationale: I came from a social link on my phone. A page that scrolls sideways feels amateur and broken in the first second, and I'll swipe back to the feed.

suggested_fix.current: Homepage content overflows viewport width at 375px (horizontal scroll)
suggested_fix.proposed: Constrain offending element(s) with max-width:100% / overflow-x clipping so the page fits the 375px viewport

finding: Homepage layout shift on load is high (CLS 0.263) — content jumps as the page settles.

evidence.quote: / ... Layout shift (CLS): 0.263

persona_rationale: Content jumping under my thumb as I land makes me mis-tap and feels unpolished, reinforcing a 'leave fast' impulse.

suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for hero image, logo strip, and stat row (explicit width/height or aspect-ratio) to bring CLS under 0.1

finding: After submitting the application, the only confirmation is a tiny green line of text beside the submit button; the form stays full, nothing is disabled, and there is no redirect or page-level success state.

evidence.quote: small green text 'Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.' to the right of SUBMIT APPLICATION, form still populated
small green inline text "Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly." appears beside the button; NO redirect, NO page-level confirmation

persona_rationale: I clicked submit and glanced away — with a 10-second attention span I'll never notice a faint line of text next to a button. The full, un-cleared form tells me it failed, so I'd resubmit or leave thinking the site is broken. This is the one action the whole site is built for.

suggested_fix.current: Inline green text beside the button; form remains populated; no redirect
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page (or replace the form area with a full-width success panel: large heading 'Application received', what happens next, and a clear next step)

finding: Submitting the apply form empty or with invalid data shows no validation errors at all, so the user cannot tell what was wrong.

evidence.quote: /apply ... empty: no visible validation errors after empty submit ... invalid: no visible validation errors on invalid data — entered values preserved

persona_rationale: If I miss a required field and nothing tells me which one, I just see 'nothing happened' and give up — I won't hunt for the problem.

suggested_fix.current: No inline validation messages on empty/invalid submit
suggested_fix.proposed: Show per-field inline errors next to each invalid required field (e.g. 'Enter your name', 'Choose a state') and focus the first error on submit

finding: The apply headline references 'Mike' by first name with no introduction for someone landing directly on /apply.

evidence.quote: APPLY TO BUILD YOUR VENDING BUSINESS WITH MIKE.

persona_rationale: If I land on /apply from a deep link I have no idea who Mike is; the personal name assumes context I don't have.

suggested_fix.current: 'Apply to build your vending business with Mike.'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Apply to build your vending business with Mike Hoffman, founder of Vendingpreneurs.' or add a one-line 'who is Mike' sub-caption

finding: The nav item 'NEWS' leads to a page whose heading is 'BLOG' — the same section is named two different things.

evidence.quote: BLOG

link "News":
      - /url: /news

persona_rationale: I clicked 'NEWS' and landed on something headed 'BLOG'. For a beat I think I clicked the wrong thing or got redirected somewhere unexpected — small but it erodes confidence the site is coherent.

suggested_fix.proposed: Pick one name; use 'NEWS' for both the nav and the page heading (or 'BLOG' for both)

finding: Above the fold, the article cards display as bare outlines / image alt-text placeholders before scrolling, so first paint of the news section looks broken or unfinished.

evidence.quote: article cards show alt text e.g. 'Top 10 Profitable Products to Stock in Your Vending Machine (Snacks, Healthy Items, Niche Products)' instead of loaded images
Above-the-fold on /news shows three empty card outlines before any scrolling ... first paint looks broken/unfinished

persona_rationale: If I open the content section to gauge if this brand is real and the cards are empty boxes with raw text, I assume the site is half-built and bounce.

suggested_fix.current: Scroll-reveal article cards render blank until scrolled into view; cover images show alt text
suggested_fix.proposed: Render cards visible by default (or with a low-opacity placeholder image), and ensure cover images load with a fallback so first paint shows real cards

finding: A news cover image fails to load with a 500 error, leaving a broken-image slot on the listing.

evidence.quote: 500 /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F67f65d37ab8dcabf1595af84%2F694bcfaf10178c3a1340c777_ChatGPT%2520Image...

persona_rationale: A broken image on the very first content page I open reads as neglect; it's a small thing that confirms my suspicion the site isn't finished.

suggested_fix.current: Cover image 500s (external website-files.com CDN URL through /_next/image)
suggested_fix.proposed: Re-host the image locally or fix the remote URL/loader config, and add an onError fallback image so a failed load never shows a broken slot

finding: Inside an article the back link is labelled 'BLOG' while the nav item that leads to the same destination is 'NEWS'.

evidence.quote: a 'BLOG' links to /news (verified via route visits)  ... a 'NEWS' links to /news

persona_rationale: Two labels for the same destination on the same screen makes me unsure where each goes, which is exactly the kind of tiny friction that makes a casual visitor disengage.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use the same label everywhere for /news

finding: Contact is not in the header navigation; the only in-app path is the footer link, so a visitor with a quick pre-application question has to scroll to the very bottom to find any way to reach a human.

evidence.quote: link is in the FOOTER only — "CONTACT US"; not in the header nav

persona_rationale: Before I hand over my details I often want to ask one quick question. If there's no visible 'Contact' up top I assume there's no easy way to reach them, which makes the whole thing feel less trustworthy.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add 'Contact' to the header nav (or a clearly labelled 'Questions? Contact us' link near the apply CTA)

finding: Top-nav links are very short touch targets (~17-20px tall), making them fiddly to tap on the phone a social-media visitor arrives with.

evidence.quote: NOTE: small touch target: a "ABOUT" 51x20px ... a "NEWS" 44x20px ... a "HOME" 43x17px

persona_rationale: Thumb-tapping a 17px-tall link on a phone means mis-taps, and every mis-tap is a reason for an impatient first-timer to give up.

suggested_fix.proposed: Increase tappable area to at least 44x44px (extra vertical padding on nav links) per touch-target guidelines
